tasks.subject: [dashboard-content] [P1] Replace DashboardPage with ScrollArea orchestrator composing status and sessions
tasks.description:
Replace the placeholder `DashboardPage` widget with the real orchestrator that composes `SystemStatusRow` and `ActiveSessionsSection` inside a `ScrollArea`. ## File to Modify `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/dashboard/ui/DashboardPage.tsx` — replace entirely. ## Implementation ```tsx import { ScrollArea } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { ActiveSessionsSection } from '@/layers/features/dashboard-sessions'; import { SystemStatusRow } from '@/layers/features/dashboard-status'; /** * Dashboard page — mission control overview composing feature sections. * Orchestrator widget that composes feature-level sections in a scrollable container. */ export function DashboardPage() { return (
); } ``` Note: In Phase 1, only `ActiveSessionsSection` and `SystemStatusRow` are wired. `NeedsAttentionSection` (above Active Sessions) and `RecentActivityFeed` (below System Status) will be added in later phases. Layout constraints: - `max-w-4xl` (896px) centers content with comfortable reading width - `space-y-8` (32px) between sections for visual breathing room - `px-6 py-8` padding from container edges - `ScrollArea` wraps the entire page for scrollable overflow ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `DashboardPage` renders `ScrollArea` with centered container - [ ] `ActiveSessionsSection` and `SystemStatusRow` are composed inside - [ ] Container uses `max-w-4xl space-y-8 px-6 py-8` - [ ] No placeholder text remains - [ ] Widget imports only from features and shared layers (FSD compliance) - [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes - [ ] Visual: page scrolls when content overflows

tasks.subject: [dashboard-content] [P3] Wire RecentActivityFeed into DashboardPage and finalize section order
tasks.description:
Add `RecentActivityFeed` to the `DashboardPage` widget as the bottom section, completing the full section order. ## File to Modify `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/dashboard/ui/DashboardPage.tsx` ## Implementation Final orchestrator with all four sections: ```tsx import { ScrollArea } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { NeedsAttentionSection } from '@/layers/features/dashboard-attention'; import { ActiveSessionsSection } from '@/layers/features/dashboard-sessions'; import { SystemStatusRow } from '@/layers/features/dashboard-status'; import { RecentActivityFeed } from '@/layers/features/dashboard-activity'; /** * Dashboard page — mission control overview composing feature sections. * Answers four questions in priority order: * 1. Does anything need my attention? * 2. What is active right now? * 3. Is the system healthy? * 4. What happened while I was away? */ export function DashboardPage() { return (
); } ``` Section order follows the signal hierarchy: 1. Needs Attention (urgent, renders nothing when empty) 2. Active Sessions (current work) 3. System Status (infrastructure health) 4. Recent Activity (historical, scrollable) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All four sections present in correct order - [ ] `RecentActivityFeed` is the last section - [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes - [ ] FSD layer imports valid - [ ] Full page scrolls with all sections populated
tasks.activeForm: Wiring RecentActivityFeed into DashboardPage and finalizing section order

tasks.subject: [dashboard-content] [P4] Add entrance animations with stagger to all dashboard sections
tasks.description:
Add consistent entrance animations to all dashboard sections using module-scope motion variants, following `contributing/animations.md` patterns.

## Shared Animation Variants

Each feature module should define its own animation variants at module scope (not inline). Use consistent values across all sections:

```typescript
// Section entrance — used by each section's root element
const sectionEntrance = {
  initial: { opacity: 0, y: 12 },
  animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 },
  transition: { duration: 0.3, ease: 'easeOut' },
} as const;

// Stagger container — used by lists of items
const staggerContainer = {
  animate: { transition: { staggerChildren: 0.04 } },
} as const;

// Stagger item — used by individual items in a list
const staggerItem = {
  initial: { opacity: 0, y: 8 },
  animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 },
} as const;
```

## Files to Update

### `ActiveSessionsSection.tsx`

Wrap section with `motion.section` using `sectionEntrance` variants. Wrap the grid with `motion.div` using `staggerContainer`, and each `ActiveSessionCard` with `motion.div` using `staggerItem`.

### `SystemStatusRow.tsx`

Wrap section with `motion.section` using `sectionEntrance`. Wrap the grid with `motion.div` using `staggerContainer`, and each card with `motion.div` using `staggerItem`.

### `NeedsAttentionSection.tsx`

Already has `AnimatePresence` and conditional animation. Verify the `conditionalSection` variant is correctly applied:

```typescript
const conditionalSection = {
  initial: { height: 0, opacity: 0 },
  animate: { height: 'auto', opacity: 1 },
  exit: { height: 0, opacity: 0 },
  transition: { duration: 0.25, ease: [0, 0, 0.2, 1] },
} as const;
```

`AnimatePresence initial={false}` ensures no animation on initial page load when empty.

### `RecentActivityFeed.tsx`

Already has stagger animation. Verify `staggerChildren: 0.03` and item variant with `duration: 0.2`.

## Key Constraints

- `MotionConfig reducedMotion="user"` already wraps the app — no per-component work needed
- Stagger limited to first 8 items per section (use `.slice(0, 8)` before mapping stagger items)
- Import `motion` from `motion/react`
- All variants at module scope, never inline

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All sections animate in with fade + y-offset on page load
- [ ] Session cards and status cards stagger in with 0.04s delay
- [ ] Needs Attention section animates height 0 → auto when items appear
- [ ] Activity feed groups stagger in with 0.03s delay
- [ ] Reduced motion preference is respected (via MotionConfig)
- [ ] No animation jank with 8+ items
- [ ] Variants defined at module scope, not inline
tasks.activeForm: Adding entrance animations with stagger to all dashboard sections

tasks.subject: [dashboard-content] [P4] Verify light/dark mode, reduced motion, and disabled subsystem states
tasks.description:
Verify and fix all visual states across the dashboard: light/dark mode, reduced motion, and disabled subsystem rendering.

## Color Semantics Verification

Verify every color usage matches the specification:

| State | Dot Color | Text Color |
|---|---|---|
| Active (running) | `bg-blue-500 animate-pulse` | — |
| Idle | `bg-muted-foreground/30` | — |
| Healthy (system) | `bg-muted-foreground/30` | `text-muted-foreground` |
| Warning (stalled, disconnected) | `bg-amber-500` | `text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-500` |
| Error (failed, unreachable) | `bg-red-500` | `text-red-600 dark:text-red-500` |
| Session completed (feed) | `bg-green-500` | — |

Note: `bg-green-500` only used in activity feed for "session completed" badge — never for system health.

## Dark Mode Verification

Check all components render correctly in both light and dark mode:
- `SubsystemCard`: border, background, text colors
- `ActiveSessionCard`: border, background, status dot colors
- `NeedsAttentionSection`: amber header text uses `text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-500`
- `SystemHealthDot`: dot colors visible against both backgrounds
- `ActivityFeedItem`: type dot colors, text colors, border accent
- Empty states: `border-dashed bg-card/50` renders correctly

## Disabled Subsystem States

Verify disabled subsystem cards render correctly:
- When `usePulseEnabled()` returns `false`: Pulse card shows title + "Disabled" in `text-muted-foreground/50`
- When `useRelayEnabled()` returns `false`: Relay card shows title + "Disabled" in `text-muted-foreground/50`
- Both disabled cards remain clickable (open their respective panels)
- Mesh card always renders (no feature gate per ADR-0062)

## Reduced Motion

- `MotionConfig reducedMotion="user"` is already at the app level
- Verify animations are disabled when user prefers reduced motion
- `animate-pulse` on active session dots should respect prefers-reduced-motion

## First-Visit "Zero State"

Verify the dashboard looks calm and ready when completely empty:
- Needs Attention: hidden (zero DOM)
- Active Sessions: shows empty state with "New session →" CTA
- System Status: shows all disabled cards with neutral styling
- Recent Activity: shows empty state message
- Overall: no broken layout, no loading spinners stuck, no error states

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All color semantics match the specification table
- [ ] All components render correctly in light and dark mode
- [ ] Disabled subsystem cards show muted "Disabled" label
- [ ] Disabled cards remain clickable
- [ ] Mesh card always renders
- [ ] Reduced motion preference is respected
- [ ] Zero state (completely empty dashboard) looks calm and intentional
- [ ] No orphaned loading states or stuck skeletons
tasks.activeForm: Verifying light/dark mode, reduced motion, and disabled subsystem states

tasks.subject: [dashboard-content] [P4] Update project-structure documentation for new feature modules
tasks.description:
Update `contributing/project-structure.md` to document the 4 new feature modules added by the dashboard-content spec.

## File to Modify

`contributing/project-structure.md`

## Changes

Add entries for the new feature modules in the features section:

```markdown
### features/dashboard-attention/
Conditional attention zone for items needing user action. Renders zero DOM when empty.
Derives attention items from sessions (stalled), Pulse (failed runs), Relay (dead letters), and Mesh (offline agents).

### features/dashboard-sessions/
Active session cards grid. Filters sessions updated within 2 hours, resolves agent identity,
and displays up to 6 session cards with active/idle status heuristic.

### features/dashboard-status/
System health cards and activity sparkline. Shows Pulse, Relay, Mesh, and Activity cards
in a responsive 4-column grid. Includes pure SVG sparkline for 7-day session counts.

### features/dashboard-activity/
Time-grouped activity event feed with last-visit tracking. Aggregates session and Pulse events
into Today/Yesterday/Last 7 days groups with a "since your last visit" separator.
```

Also update the `AGENTS.md` client section if it lists dashboard components — add a note about the four content sections:

```markdown
- `/` → `DashboardPage` (widgets/dashboard) — mission control with four sections:
  NeedsAttentionSection, ActiveSessionsSection, SystemStatusRow, RecentActivityFeed.
  With `DashboardSidebar` (navigation + recent agents) and `DashboardHeader` (health dot + quick actions).
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `contributing/project-structure.md` documents all 4 new feature modules
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` client section updated with dashboard content description
- [ ] Documentation is accurate and consistent with implementation
- [ ] No stale placeholder references remain
tasks.activeForm: Updating project-structure documentation for new feature modules
